unit.name: wpdb result population boundary live database Haxe candidate
unit.public_contract: WordPress-compatible maybe_create_table(), maybe_add_column(), dbDelta(), and wpdb::query/get_var/get_row/get_results remain callable with their public PHP class/function shape while selected schema, dbDelta, and result-population decisions delegate to typed Haxe and continue to execute against locked live MySQL and MariaDB runtimes through wpdb.

bridge.reason: WordPress core and plugins call global PHP upgrade helpers and wpdb methods with native PHP signatures, mutable public state, and stdClass/array result contracts. The candidate keeps those public PHP shells and delegates normalized table/column existence, dbDelta planning, and bounded result-population decisions to Haxe.
bounded_by: WPHX-305.14 live MySQL/MariaDB oracle comparison receipt
native mysqli-backed wpdb query boundary for real database execution

removal_gate.condition: Promote from generated shell candidate to verified owned distribution only after broader wpdb/schema distribution work proves include timing, reflection, mixed PHP ABI, plugin compatibility, and wpdb replacement/drop-in behavior.

smell_fixes.description: Separated maybe_create_table()/maybe_add_column() branch decisions, bounded dbDelta classification, wpdb result-shape/null decisions, wpdb::query return/state branch decisions, _do_query timing/logging/execution/reconnect policy, and selected-row last_result/num_rows population policy from native SQL execution into typed Haxe helpers, making the decision surface explicit without changing observable WordPress behavior.

notes: The candidate provisions mysql-8.4 and mariadb-11.8 containers from locked index digests and runs the WPHX-305.07 live DB cases plus WPHX-305.11 result-shape, WPHX-305.12 query-state, WPHX-305.13 native-execution, and WPHX-305.14 result-population cases with local PHP mysqli. The broad wp-admin include remains stubbed because this workset is scoped to database helpers, dbDelta planning decisions, wpdb result population, and storage paths.
